title: Path-based JSON value access
description: pjson must support path-based addressing for reading, writing, and deleting JSON values. Paths follow a slash-separated format (e.g., '/a/b/0/c') where numeric segments address array elements and string segments address object keys.
rationale: Path-based access is essential for database-like operations on hierarchical JSON structures.
author: netkeep80
acceptance_criteria: get(path) retrieves a value at the specified path
put(path, value) creates or updates a value at the specified path
erase(path) removes a value at the specified path
Intermediate containers are auto-created on put
